intro: You can request repeat prescriptions for chronic medications 24 hours a day (including weekends) in 2 ways:
patientenomgeving: You can request repeat prescriptions online via the patient environment on the website. However, you must register first. Please note that it may take up to 7 days after creating an account before it becomes active.
receptenlijn: Call your own GP's practice telephone number and select the prescription line option from the menu.
receptenlijnVragen: Name and date of birth of the person the prescription is for
Street and house number of your home address
Name or names of the medicines you are ordering (keep the boxes handy!)
welkeMedicijnen:
You can only request prescriptions for medicines that you use on the advice of your GP or specialist chronically (usually daily).

If you want to request medicines for newly occurring complaints or temporary medicines -- that you received for a specific period -- and wish to reorder them, you cannot order these medicines not via the prescription line or website. For this you can call the practice assistant during office hours; if necessary, your GP will be consulted about your request.
wanneerKlaar: If you leave a message on the prescription line or order a prescription via the website before 12:00 noon, you can 2 working days later collect your medicines from your own pharmacy (they will be ready from 15:00).
wanneerKlaarVoorbeelden: If you call/email Tuesday evening, your medicines will be ready at the pharmacy on Friday.
If you call/email Friday afternoon after 12:00 or during the weekend, you can collect your medicines on Wednesday after 15:00.
